sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Neither DOWN nor LEFT moved anything. Let me check segmentation to understand current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Current objects:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:  # skip small objects
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check if there's a pattern - maybe I need to use SPACE or MOUSE
# Let me also check history to see when the black shape last moved
print(f"\nTotal nodes: {len(nodes)}")
print(f"Step: {current_frame.step}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Now I see the current state more clearly:
- The black L-shape (id=10) is at boundary [[18, 45], [18, 53], [26, 53], [27, 54], [27, 59], [29, 59], [29, 51], [21, 51], [20, 50], [20, 45]]
- The yellow L-shape (id=27) is at boundary [[42, 9], [42, 17], [44, 17], [44, 12], [45, 11], [53, 11], [53, 3], [51, 3], [51, 8], [50, 9]]
The black shape is at rows 18-29, cols 45-59.
The yellow shape is at rows 42-53, cols 3-17.
Neither LEFT nor DOWN moved the black shape. This is strange. Let me check the history to see when the black shape was last at a different position. Maybe it moved earlier and is now stuck.
Wait - looking at the history, I moved LEFT 14 times earlier and the black shape moved from cols 59-73 to cols 45-59. So it DID move before. But now it's not moving anymore.
Maybe the black shape is blocked by something? Or maybe there's a limit to how far it can move? Or maybe I need to use a different action to "activate" movement?
Let me check the history frames to see what the black shape's position was before. I need to look at earlier frames to understand when it stopped moving.
Actually, let me try RIGHT to see if the black shape can move in any direction at all.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check history to see where the black shape was before
# and try RIGHT to see if it can move at all
for entry in history[-10:]:
    seg = entry.frame.segmentation
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] == 64:
            print(f"Step {entry.frame.step}: black L at boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Try RIGHT to test if movement still works
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
